commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.3: The Nesting Chain — one recursion motif, four nested forms (#16)

Give the Nesting Chain movement a cohesive signature by propagating the
depth node's strongest element — glowing concentric self-similar frames —
backward through the three earlier nodes, so the whole chain reads as one
escalating statement about containment, culminating in the infinite dive.

Each node now visualises 'a thing that holds a smaller copy of itself',
in a form distinct from its siblings and from every other section:

- scaleword: the punch-word lands inside concentric frame-echoes that
  ignite outer->inner as the node scrolls — the nest assembling behind the
  words, foreshadowing the dive. (Was: a lone word in a field of dead space.)
- ring: recast from a cliche loading dial into concentric NESTED rings that
  fill in turn, the centre counting how deep the nesting has reached — the
  containment idea in the round, no longer colliding with the gauge dial.
- shapemorph: the square now literally holds a smaller copy of itself, three
  deep, all morphing in lockstep — the caption's 'holds another' made true.
  Hue kept in the iris/violet family so it never drifts off the movement's palette.
- depth: unchanged — the climax the others now echo.

Treatments live entirely in each painter (build+paint) + its CSS block,
selected by node.kind; the shared paint loop and frame chrome stay generic.
Colour threads through --acc-iris; the scaleword pop stays gated on
REDUCE_MOTION. Verified in Chrome at 1280px and 390px: zero console
errors/warnings across full forward+reverse scrub, clean scrub, no duplication.
